channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The best games of 2026 so far, picked by NPR's staff
item.description: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.content:encoded: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
item.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.
item.content:encoded:
The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

(Image credit: Shekhar Yadav)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
item.description: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.
item.content:encoded:
Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

(Image credit: Mohammed Huwais)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
item.description: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How soccer brings a Bhutanese community together in Harrisburg, Pa.
item.description: Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.
item.content:encoded:
Harrisburg is home to one of the nation's largest communities of Nepali-speaking Bhutanese refugees and their families. Through soccer, generations have found connection, belonging and a way to carry forward a shared history.

(Image credit: Maansi Srivastava)
item.dc:creator: Maansi Srivastava

item.title: The best economic ideas from around the world — Planet Money Summer School is back
item.description: Our free economics course for your ears is on a quest for great economics ideas.
item.content:encoded: Our free economics course for your ears is on a quest for great economics ideas.
item.dc:creator: Planet Money

item.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
item.description: The Houthis, a Shiite militia in Yemen, which is aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait.
item.content:encoded:
The Houthis, a Shiite militia in Yemen, which is aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab Al-Mandeb Strait.

(Image credit: Mohammed Huwais)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
item.description: Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.
item.content:encoded:
Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.

(Image credit: Andi Rice for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: A homeless man was charged with a felony for camping. He's one of the first
item.description: When Joseph Lamont Williams refused to leave the public park where he has lived for years, he was charged with a felony. Camping bans are on the rise as states seek to crack down on homelessness.
item.content:encoded:
When Joseph Lamont Williams refused to leave the public park where he has lived for years, he was charged with a felony. Camping bans are on the rise as states seek to crack down on homelessness.

(Image credit: Erica Lynn for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marissa J. Lang

item.title: Arizona primaries set the stage for contested fall races in a swing state
item.description: Arizona Democrats choose who can try to flip a Republican-held House seat, and Republicans pick their nominee to try to unseat a Democratic governor.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona Democrats choose who can try to flip a Republican-held House seat, and Republicans pick their nominee to try to unseat a Democratic governor.

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
